title: The Tailor Shop
introKo:
결혼식 하객 정장을 맞추러 간 양복점, 유난히 꼼꼼한 재단사 Mr. Pin의 이야기.
…표면적으로는요.

quiz.q: Mr. Pin이 무릎까지 꿇고 잰 것은요?

normal.text: Your best friend is getting married next month. You need a suit, so you visit a small tailor shop on a quiet street.
The tailor, Mr. Pin, welcomes you with a warm smile. People say no one in town measures as carefully as he does.
"A wedding suit must fit like a second skin," he says. "Please step up. First, I need to learn your measurements — all of them."

options.en: Sure. It's my first time getting a suit made.
options.reaction: "A first time is special," he says. "Relax and leave everything to me."

options.en: Okay. How long will this take?
options.reaction: "Good work takes time," he smiles. "I never rush the important parts."

normal.text: He wraps his tape measure around your chest, then your waist. "Breathe out slowly," he says. "Don't hold anything back."
"Nice and snug," he murmurs, pulling the tape a little closer. "The tape never lies. Your numbers are very good."

options.en: Is it supposed to be this tight?
options.reaction: "Snug is not tight," he says. "A good suit should hold you close all day."

options.en: You are very thorough, Mr. Pin.
options.reaction: "Always," he says. "I take my time with every inch."

normal.text: "Now, the inseam," he says, and he kneels in front of you. "Stand still. This part needs a steady hand."
He runs the tape up along the inside of your leg. "Almost done," he says. "Numbers must be honest."
Then he drapes soft fabric over your shoulders and pins it, piece by piece. "Hold still," he says. "Feel how it flows over the body."

options.reaction: "It should. Good fabric touches everything and hides nothing."

options.en: Will it be ready before the wedding?
options.reaction: "Of course. But come back for a second fitting. The second time is always better than the first."

normal.text: At last, he steps back and looks at you in the mirror. "You see?" he says. "By now, this suit knows your body better than you do."

options.en: I might have to come back for more fittings.
options.ko: 가봉하러 또 와야 할 것 같아요.
options.reaction: "I was hoping you would say that," he says. "My door is always open for you."

options.en: Thank you. I think it is perfect just as it is.
options.reaction: "Then my work here is done," he says. "For now."

normal.text: On the wedding day, everyone asks where you got your suit. You just smile. Some things stay between you and your tailor.

hard.text: Your best friend is getting married next month, which means you finally need a proper suit. You find a small tailor shop tucked away on a quiet street.
The tailor, Mr. Pin, welcomes you with a knowing smile. People around town swear that nobody takes measurements as carefully as he does.
"A wedding suit must fit like a second skin," he declares. "Step up onto the platform, please. Before anything else, I must learn your measurements — every last one."

options.en: Sure. It's my first time having a suit made.
options.reaction: "A first time is always special," he says softly. "Relax, and leave everything in my hands."

options.en: Okay. Roughly how long will this take?
options.reaction: "Good work cannot be rushed," he smiles. "And I never hurry over the important parts."

hard.text: He wraps the tape measure around your chest, then lets it slide down to your waist. "Breathe out slowly," he says. "Don't hold anything back from me."
"Nice and snug," he murmurs, drawing the tape a touch closer. "The tape never lies, and I must say, your numbers are excellent."

options.en: Is it supposed to feel this tight?
options.reaction: "Snug is not the same as tight," he explains. "A proper suit should hold you close from morning until night."

options.en: You are remarkably thorough, Mr. Pin.
options.reaction: "Always," he says. "I always take my time with every single inch."

hard.text: "Now for the inseam," he says, kneeling down in front of you. "Stand perfectly still. This particular measurement calls for a steady hand."
He runs the tape up along the inside of your leg. "Almost there," he says. "An honest number is worth a little patience."
Then he drapes soft fabric across your shoulders, pinning it into place piece by piece. "Hold still," he warns. "Feel how it flows over the body — that is how you judge good fabric."

options.reaction: "It should. Fine fabric touches everything and hides nothing."

options.en: Will it be finished before the wedding?
options.reaction: "Naturally. But you must return for a second fitting — the second time is always better than the first."

hard.text: At last he steps back and looks at you in the mirror. "You see?" he says with quiet pride. "By the end, the suit knows your body better than you know it yourself."

options.en: I suspect I will need to come back for more fittings.
options.ko: 가봉하러 또 와야 할 것 같아요.
options.reaction: "I was rather hoping you would say that," he replies. "My door will always be open for you."

options.en: Thank you. Honestly, I think it is perfect just as it is.
options.ko: 고맙습니다. 솔직히 이대로도 완벽한 것 같아요.
options.reaction: "Then my work here is finished," he says. "For now, at least."

hard.text: On the wedding day, everyone wants to know where you found such a suit. You simply smile. Some things stay between you and your tailor.

0000.name: 줄자만 스친 손님
0000.nameEn: A Brief Word with the Tape
0000.en: How long, is it too tight, will it be ready — those were your only questions, and once the suit fit, you called it perfect and left. Mr. Pin folds his tape with a quiet nod. Some visitors come only for a suit, and he sends them off with a polite bow.
0000.ko: 얼마나 걸리는지, 너무 조이는 건 아닌지, 제때 되는지 — 당신의 질문은 그게 전부였고, 옷이 맞자 완벽하다며 떠났어요. Mr. Pin은 줄자를 조용히 접으며 고개를 끄덕여요. 어떤 손님은 정장 하나만 얻으러 오고, 그는 정중한 인사로 보내 주죠.

0001.name: 뒤늦게 문을 돌아본 손님
0001.nameEn: A Second Glance at the Door
0001.en: You were all business at first — how long, is it too tight, will it be ready — yet at the very end you hinted you might come back. Mr. Pin lifted an eyebrow, pleased. The careful ones, he decides, always return when they least expect to.
0001.ko: 처음엔 온통 실무적이었죠. 얼마나 걸리는지, 너무 조이는 건 아닌지, 제때 되는지. 그런데 마지막에 또 오겠다는 말을 흘렸어요. Mr. Pin은 흐뭇하게 눈썹을 올려요. 신중한 분들이 꼭, 스스로도 예상 못 한 순간에 다시 온다고, 그는 생각해요.

0010.name: 원단에 마음 뺏긴 손님
0010.en: You watched the clock and worried about the fit, but when the fabric touched your shoulders, you forgot yourself and said how wonderful it felt. Then you took your suit and went. Mr. Pin smiles at the empty platform. The cloth, he murmurs, always speaks louder than he does.
0010.ko: 시계를 보고 핏을 걱정하던 당신이었지만, 원단이 어깨에 닿자 그만 넋을 놓고 감촉이 참 좋다고 했어요. 그러고는 정장을 챙겨 떠났죠. Mr. Pin은 빈 단을 보며 미소 지어요. 천이란 게 늘 자기보다 말을 잘한다고, 그가 나직이 중얼거려요.

0011.name: 감촉을 잊지 못한 단골
0011.en: You started with practical questions, but the fabric won you over, and by the end you were already planning your next visit. Mr. Pin knew the look. Once someone feels good cloth, he says, a single suit is never enough.
0011.ko: 실무적인 질문으로 시작했지만 원단에 마음을 뺏겼고, 끝날 무렵엔 벌써 다음 방문을 계획하고 있었죠. Mr. Pin은 그 표정을 알아봐요. 좋은 천을 한번 느끼고 나면 정장 한 벌로는 절대 성에 안 찬다고, 그가 말해요.

0100.name: 꼼꼼함을 알아본 손님
0100.en: You kept things practical, but you did tell him how wonderfully thorough he was — and he took that to heart. The suit was ready on time, and you wore it once, beautifully. A compliment like that, Mr. Pin says, keeps a tailor warm all winter.
0100.ko: 당신은 대체로 실무적이었지만, 참 꼼꼼하시다는 말만은 건넸고, 그는 그 말을 마음에 담았어요. 정장은 제때 완성됐고, 당신은 그 옷을 딱 한 번, 아주 멋지게 입었죠. 그런 칭찬 한마디면 재단사는 겨우내 따뜻하다고, Mr. Pin이 말해요.

0101.name: 다시 찾을 줄 알았던 손님
0101.en: You admired how careful he was and, sure enough, you asked to come back before you even left. Mr. Pin was not surprised in the slightest. People who notice the details, he says, are the ones who keep coming for more of them.
0101.ko: 당신은 그의 세심함에 감탄했고, 아니나 다를까 나가기도 전에 또 오겠다고 했어요. Mr. Pin은 조금도 놀라지 않았죠. 디테일을 알아보는 분들이 그 디테일을 더 누리러 자꾸 찾아온다고, 그가 말해요.

0110.name: 칭찬만 남기고 간 손님
0110.en: You praised his careful hands and the wonderful fabric both, and Mr. Pin glowed at every word — then you thanked him and walked out for good. He stands a while by the mirror. So generous with his compliments, he sighs, and so quick with his goodbye.
0110.ko: 당신은 그의 세심한 손길도, 근사한 원단도 아낌없이 칭찬했고, Mr. Pin은 한마디 한마디에 환해졌어요. 그러고는 고맙다는 인사와 함께 영영 떠났죠. 그는 한참 거울 곁에 서 있어요. 칭찬엔 그렇게 후하시더니 작별은 어찌 그리 빠르신지, 하고 한숨지어요.

0111.name: 말끝마다 다음을 남긴 손님
0111.en: Once you warmed up, you could not stop: how thorough he was, how the fabric felt, and how you simply had to come back. Mr. Pin marked your measurements in his best notebook. Some customers, he says, you write down in ink.
0111.ko: 한번 마음이 풀리자 당신은 멈추질 못했어요. 얼마나 꼼꼼하신지, 원단 감촉이 어떤지, 그리고 꼭 다시 와야겠다는 말까지. Mr. Pin은 아끼는 수첩에 당신의 치수를 적어 뒀어요. 어떤 손님은 지워지지 않게 펜으로 적어 둔다고, 그가 말해요.

1000.name: 첫걸음만 대담했던 손님
1000.en: You strode in and told him to lead the way — then went quiet, wondered if it was too tight, and only asked that it be ready in time. Mr. Pin found it charming. The bold ones, he says, often turn shy the moment the tape comes out.
1000.ko: 당신은 성큼 들어와 다 맡기겠다고 했죠. 그러더니 조용해져서 너무 조이는 건 아닌지 묻고, 제때만 되면 된다고 했어요. Mr. Pin은 그게 귀여웠어요. 대담하게 들어온 분들이 줄자만 나오면 수줍어진다고, 그가 말해요.

1001.name: 조용히 단골이 된 손님
1001.en: You breezed in trusting him with everything, stayed shy through the fitting, and slipped in a quiet request to return. Mr. Pin liked that best of all. The loud ones tell everyone, he says. The quiet ones simply come back.
1001.ko: 당신은 다 맡기겠다며 가볍게 들어와, 가봉 내내 수줍게 있다가, 또 오겠다는 말을 슬쩍 흘렸어요. Mr. Pin은 그게 제일 좋았대요. 요란한 분들은 온 동네에 떠들지만 조용한 분들은 그냥 다시 온다고, 그가 말해요.

1010.name: 원단 앞에서 무너진 손님
1010.en: You came in cool and confident, and though the tape made you a little shy, the fabric undid you completely — you told him it felt wonderful before catching yourself. Then you left without a word about returning. The cloth, Mr. Pin muses, gets a confession out of everyone.
1010.ko: 당신은 여유롭고 자신 있게 들어왔고, 줄자 앞에선 조금 수줍었지만, 원단 앞에선 완전히 무너졌어요. 정신 차리기도 전에 감촉이 참 좋다고 해 버렸죠. 그러고는 다시 오겠다는 말 없이 떠났어요. 천이란 게 누구한테서든 고백 하나쯤은 받아 낸다고, Mr. Pin이 혼잣말해요.

1011.name: 감촉에 사로잡힌 단골
1011.en: You trusted him from the first hello, melted at the touch of the fabric, and left already asking when you could return. Only the tape measure kept you modest. Confident at the door, soft at the cloth, Mr. Pin says — his favorite kind of visitor.
1011.ko: 당신은 첫인사부터 그를 믿었고, 원단의 감촉에 녹아내렸으며, 언제 다시 와도 되냐고 물으며 떠났어요. 오직 줄자 앞에서만 얌전해졌죠. 문 앞에선 자신만만, 천 앞에선 사르르 — 제가 제일 좋아하는 손님이라고, Mr. Pin이 말해요.

1100.name: 손발이 잘 맞은 손님
1100.en: You told him to take the lead and then praised how thorough he was — the two of you worked like old partners. Still, you only needed the one suit, ready in time. A pity, Mr. Pin says. We measured so well together.
1100.ko: 당신은 다 맡기겠다더니 얼마나 꼼꼼하시냐며 칭찬까지 했고, 두 사람은 오랜 파트너처럼 척척 맞았어요. 그래도 당신에겐 제때 완성된 정장 한 벌이면 충분했죠. 아쉽네요, 하고 Mr. Pin이 말해요. 치수 하나는 참 잘 맞았는데 말이죠.

1101.name: 손발 맞는 단골이 된 손님
1101.en: You let him lead, cheered on his careful hands, and booked your next visit before the suit was even pressed. Mr. Pin already had your winter coat in mind. When two people measure this well, he says, one suit is only the beginning.
1101.ko: 당신은 그에게 다 맡기고, 세심한 손길을 응원했으며, 정장을 다리기도 전에 다음 방문을 예약했어요. Mr. Pin은 벌써 당신의 겨울 코트를 떠올리고 있었죠. 두 사람 치수가 이렇게 잘 맞으면 정장 한 벌은 그저 시작일 뿐이라고, 그가 말해요.

1110.name: 완벽했지만 떠난 손님
1110.en: You were bold from hello, generous with your praise, and lost in the feel of the fabric — the whole fitting went like a dream. Then you called it perfect and never came again. Mr. Pin keeps your measurements anyway, just in case the dream walks back in.
1110.ko: 당신은 첫인사부터 대담했고, 칭찬에 후했으며, 원단의 감촉에 흠뻑 빠졌어요. 가봉 내내 꿈처럼 흘러갔죠. 그러고는 완벽하다는 말과 함께 다시는 오지 않았어요. 그래도 Mr. Pin은 당신의 치수를 남겨 둬요. 혹시 그 꿈이 다시 걸어 들어올까 봐서요.

1111.name: 소문난 양복점의 단골
1111.nameEn: The Talk of the Tailor Shop
1111.en: By autumn the whole town knows Mr. Pin has a favorite. You trusted him completely, praised his every touch, adored the fabric, and now you return so often the platform seems to wait for you. Some suits fit the body, he tells his friends. This one fit the man. And his door, the neighbors notice, is never quite closed to you.
1111.ko: 가을 무렵엔 온 동네가 Mr. Pin에게 각별한 단골이 생겼다는 걸 알아요. 당신은 그를 완전히 믿었고, 손길마다 칭찬했으며, 원단을 사랑했고, 이제는 너무 자주 찾아와 그 단이 당신을 기다리는 것만 같아요. 어떤 정장은 몸에 맞지요, 하고 그가 친구들에게 말해요. 이 정장은 사람에게 맞았답니다. 그리고 이웃들은 눈치채요. 그의 가게 문이 당신에게만은 늘 살짝 열려 있다는 걸요.

friend.en: a person you like and trust

month.en: a period of about thirty days

visit.en: to go and see a place

tailor.en: a person who makes clothes fit

mr.en: a polite title for a man

pin.ko: 핀, 시침핀; 핀으로 고정하다 (여기서는 재단사 이름)
pin.en: a thin fastener; also the tailor's name

know.en: to have information in your head

leave.en: to put something in someone's care

tape.en: a long strip used for measuring

chest.en: the upper front of your body

breathe.en: to take air in and out

lies.en: says things that are not true

number.en: a figure such as one or two

difference.en: the way two things are unlike

between.en: in the space separating two things

inseam.en: the inside leg length of trousers

leg.en: the body part you walk with

shoulders.en: the body parts beside your neck

fitting.en: a try-on to check the fit

tucked.ko: (tucked away) 안쪽에 자리 잡은, 숨어 있는
tucked.en: hidden away in a quiet spot

platform.en: a raised place to stand on

rushed.ko: 재촉된, 급히 이뤄진 (rush의 과거분사; cannot be rushed: 서두른다고 되는 게 아니다)

touch.en: a touch means a little bit

taking.en: taking your time means going slowly

across.en: from one side to the other

judge.en: to decide how good something is

hang.en: to put up on a hook

window.en: the glass front of a shop

usual.en: normal; happening most of the time

missed.en: felt sad because someone was away

admired.en: past form of admire; looked at with pleasure

adored.en: past form of adore; loved very much

booked.en: past form of book; saved a time

bow.en: a polite bend of the head

business.en: work; all business means very serious

case.en: a situation; just in case means to be safe

catching.en: -ing form of catch; catching yourself means stopping yourself

cheered.en: past form of cheer; gave support

clock.ko: 시계 (watch the clock: 시간을 신경 쓰다)
clock.en: a thing that shows the time

dream.en: what you see in your mind while asleep

empty.en: with nothing on or in it

expect.ko: 예상하다 (least expect: 가장 예상 못 하다)

eyebrow.en: the line of hair above the eye

folds.en: third-person form of fold; bends over

forgot.ko: 잊었다 (forget의 과거; forgot yourself: 넋을 놓다)

glowed.en: past form of glow; shone with warmth

goodbye.en: what you say when you leave

hello.en: what you say when you meet someone

hinted.en: past form of hint; said in an indirect way

least.ko: 가장 덜 (least expect: 가장 예상 못 한)

left.en: past form of leave; went away

lost.en: lost in something means fully absorbed in it

marked.en: past form of mark; wrote down

mind.en: your thoughts; in mind means being thought of

muses.en: third-person form of muse; says thoughtfully

nod.en: a small down movement of the head

notebook.en: a small book to write in

notice.en: to see and become aware of

off.en: away; send off means to see someone leave

ones.en: people or things of a kind

praise.en: words that say something is good

pressed.en: past form of press; ironed flat

quite.en: fully; not quite means almost but not

slightest.ko: 아주 조금의 (not in the slightest: 조금도 ~않다)

slipped.en: past form of slip; put in quietly

strode.en: past form of stride; walked with long steps

surprised.en: feeling something you did not expect

suspect.en: to think something is likely true

undid.en: past form of undo; took apart

visitor.en: a person who comes to visit

warmed.en: past form of warm; warmed up means became relaxed

won.en: past form of win; won over means charmed

wink.inseam: 안쪽 다리 길이. 재단사가 재는 부위 중 가장 민망한 곳이라, 어른들 농담에 단골로 등장해요.
wink.snug: 몸에 딱 맞아 아늑한. 옷 얘기지만, 두 사람의 거리가 지나치게 가까울 때도 이렇게 말해요.
wink.fit: 몸에 꼭 맞다. 옷이 아니라 두 사람이 잘 맞는다는 뜻으로 들릴 수도 있어요.
wink.pin: 핀으로 고정하다. 상대를 움직이지 못하게 붙잡아 둔다는 뜻도 있어서, 재단사 이름치고는 꽤 의미심장해요.
wink.measure: 치수를 재다. 온몸 구석구석 손이 가는 일이라, 이 가게에서는 줄자가 재는 게 치수만이 아닌 것처럼 들려요.
wink.kneels: 무릎을 꿇다. 치수 잴 때 재단사의 정석 자세지만, 다른 이야기에서는 청혼처럼 아주 간절한 순간에 나오는 자세이기도 해요.
wink.kneeling: 무릎을 꿇다. 치수 잴 때 재단사의 정석 자세지만, 다른 이야기에서는 청혼처럼 아주 간절한 순간에 나오는 자세이기도 해요.
wink.tape: 줄자. 몸을 감고 살짝 조이는 물건이라, 행간에서는 손길처럼 읽히기도 해요.
wink.flows: 흐르다. 원단이 몸을 따라 흐른다는 표현은, 무언가가 피부 위를 스쳐 가는 장면을 떠올리게 해요.
wink.second: 두 번째. 두 번째 가봉이 처음보다 낫다는 말이지만, 원어민 귀에는 다른 종류의 '두 번째'부터 떠올라요.

winkEn.inseam: The one measurement that goes all the way up the leg. Tailors are professional about it; comedians never are.
winkEn.snug: Tailor-speak for a close fit. Also how you might describe two people standing closer than strictly necessary.
winkEn.fit: Clothes can fit like a second skin. So can two people, if you let the phrase wander.
winkEn.pin: To pin someone is to hold them in place so they cannot move. Quite a name for a man this hands-on.
winkEn.measure: Taking your measurements is his job. The way he says it, the tape seems curious about more than numbers.
winkEn.kneels: Perfectly normal tailoring posture. Still, kneeling in front of someone usually signals a very different kind of question.
winkEn.kneeling: Standard for tailors at work. Outside the shop, going down on one knee tends to mean a proposal, or pure devotion.
winkEn.tape: It wraps around you and pulls you a little closer. Between the lines, it reads less like a tool and more like a touch.
winkEn.flows: How fine fabric moves over a body. The picture it paints is of something gliding along bare skin.
winkEn.second: 'The second time is always better than the first.' He means fittings. The sentence has other well-known applications.

introEn:
A suit fitting with Mr. Pin, a remarkably thorough tailor.
...on the surface.
